meaning: adj.可公开得,(指理由等)表面的,虚假的
examples: The ostensible reason for his absence was illness.
The ostensible purpose of these meetings was to gather information on financial strategies.
The ostensible goal is tomove much of the processing from the PC to the Internet.
Mitchell, is preaching the power of "services"--a familiar bromide and ostensible refuge for commodity industries.
The ostensible trigger was the sacking of the Free Democrat economics and transport minister, Istvan Csillag.
